skill.name: product-changelog
skill.description: Product changelog and release notes that users actually read. Covers categorization, user-facing language, visuals, and distribution. Use for: release notes, changelogs, product updates, feature announcements, versioning. Triggers: changelog, release notes, product update, version notes, what's new, feature announcement, product changelog, update log, release announcement, version release, product release, ship notes
skill.summary: Product changelog and release notes that users actually read. Covers categorization, user-facing lan...
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: All 33 static findings are false positives. The skill is a pure documentation/writing tool for creating product changelogs. The detected patterns (external commands, network URLs, cryptographic algorithms) are actually documentation content, example CLI commands, version numbers, and URLs referenced in usage guides - not executable code or security risks.

content.user_title: Write Product Changelogs Users Actually Read
content.value_statement: Product teams struggle to create changelogs that engage users. This skill provides templates, categorization rules, and best practices for writing release notes that drive user engagement and communicate value clearly.
actual_capabilities: Generate user-facing changelog entries with clear benefit statements
Apply proper categorization (New, Improved, Fixed, Removed, Security)
Structure full changelog pages with date-based organization
Write breaking change announcements with migration timelines
Create social media snippets for feature announcements
Format version numbers using semantic versioning rules
limitations: Does not generate actual release notes content from commit history
Does not integrate with version control systems
Does not publish or distribute changelogs automatically
Requires manual input of feature details and changes

use_cases.title: SaaS Product Manager
use_cases.description: Create weekly release notes for a B2B SaaS product to keep users informed about new features and improvements.

use_cases.title: Developer Advocate
use_cases.description: Write clear, engaging changelog entries that translate technical changes into user value.

use_cases.title: Open Source Maintainer
use_cases.description: Document releases for open source projects with proper versioning and breaking change notices.

prompt_templates.title: New Feature Entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a changelog entry for a new feature called '[Feature Name]' that allows users to [what users can do]. Include the benefit and a link to documentation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a single changelog entry for a new feature

prompt_templates.title: Full Release Changelog
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a complete changelog for version [X.Y.Z] released on [Date]. Include: [list of changes with categories]. Use proper categorization and user-facing language.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating a complete release notes page

prompt_templates.title: Breaking Change Notice
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a breaking change announcement for [feature/API] being deprecated. Include: what changed, what users need to do, timeline, and migration guide link.

prompt_templates.title: Social Media Announcement
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a social media (Twitter/X) announcement for a new feature. Include: emoji, feature name, 1-2 sentence description, and call to action.
prompt_templates.scenario: Promoting a feature on social media

output_examples.input: Write a changelog entry for bulk export feature
output:
### New: Bulk Export for Reports 📊

You can now export up to 10,000 rows at once from any report view. Select your rows, click Export, and choose CSV or Excel format.

Previously limited to 500 rows per export.

output_examples.input: Create breaking change notice for API v1 deprecation
output:
### ⚠️ Breaking: API v1 Endpoints Deprecated

**What changed:** API v1 endpoints will stop working on March 15, 2026.

**What you need to do:**
1. Update your API calls to use v2 endpoints
2. Update authentication to use Bearer tokens instead of API keys
3. Test your integration before March 15

best_practices: Start each entry with what the user can do, not what was built
Group changes by category (New, Improved, Fixed, Removed, Security)
Include visual elements for major UI features
anti_patterns: Using internal developer language that users cannot understand
Writing vague entries like 'various bug fixes and improvements'
Listing breaking changes without clear migration instructions

faq.question: What is the difference between 'New' and 'Improved' categories?
faq.answer: Use 'New' for entirely new capabilities users could not do before. Use 'Improved' when existing features work better, faster, or have new options.

faq.question: How often should I publish a changelog?
faq.answer: SaaS products with continuous deployment should batch changes weekly. Major feature launches deserve immediate changelogs with blog posts.

faq.question: Should I include bug fixes in my changelog?
faq.answer: Only include bug fixes that affect user experience. Fixes for obscure edge cases or internal issues should be skipped.

faq.question: How do I handle breaking changes?
faq.answer: Place breaking changes prominently with clear warnings. Include what changed, what users must do, and a timeline with migration guide.

faq.question: Can this skill generate changelogs from git commits?
faq.answer: No. This skill helps you write user-facing changelog entries. You provide the feature details and this skill formats them properly.

faq.question: What tools work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for generating and refining changelog content.
